articles.title: Brian Cornell (TGT) receives stock award as Target withholds shares for taxes
articles.summary: Target executive Brian C. Cornell received 50,777 shares of common stock as a performance unit award under the company's 2020 Long-Term Incentive Plan. Subsequently, 21,697 shares were withheld by Target at $120.76 per share to cover tax obligations. This Form 4 filing indicates a routine equity compensation event and tax withholding, rather than open-market trading by the executive.

articles.title: Walmart shoppers hit with new bag ban policy – and US state adds $500 fines
articles.summary: Beaufort, South Carolina, is proposing new legislation to ban all plastic bags and foam products, impacting retailers like Walmart and Target. The updated policy aims to close loopholes from a 2018 ban by mandating cloth reusable bags with stitched handles. Businesses violating the new rules could face fines up to $500 and potential revocation of their business licenses.

articles.title: Target Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
articles.summary: Target Corp. (TGT) stock rallied 1.46% to $122.21 on Monday, outperforming key market indexes like the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The company's stock closed 3.01% below its 52-week high of $126.00, achieved on March 4th. This strong performance occurred during an overall positive trading session for the stock market.
